untranslatable_reason: Signal requires trader conviction in directional thesis (subjective), foundational bearish/bullish thesis lookback (requires external signal state), and subjective panic identification (media hysteria, capitulation sentiment). These cannot be expressed using only the allowed price/technical indicators. hvol_20 approximates volatility spike, and returns approximate the 5% move threshold, but the thesis conviction and panic sentiment components are fundamentally untranslatable to mechanical rules.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture core mechanic: (1) volatility spike via hvol_20 > 2.0 (approximating 2σ threshold), (2) contrarian moves via returns exceeding 5% in either direction. Exit uses 15% stop loss as specified. However, this signal fundamentally depends on trader discretion regarding thesis conviction and subjective panic assessment, which cannot be mechanized without external sentiment data or state variables tracking thesis direction.
